title: The Christian and Smoking
summary: “What? know ye not that your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost which is in you, which ye have of God, and ye are not your own? For ye are bought with a price: therefore glorify God in your body, and in your spirit, which are God's.” (1Cor 6:19-20). According to the Apostle Paul, our bodies are not our own; they belong to the Lord Jesus Christ. The price the Lord paid is his own blood. Since our bodies are borrowed from the Lord, we need to seek his counsel in all things regarding the Christian body. Our topic today is smoking. Fortunately, although it took several decades, even the secular world understands the dangers of smoking. Cigarette packets by law display the warning “Smoking kills”. Someone has said that smoking is slow suicide. Paul declared the body to be the temple of the Holy Ghost. “If any man defile the temple of God, him shall God destroy; for the temple of God is holy, which temple ye are” (I Cor 3:17). There is a tragic incident recorded in the Old Testament which underscores this truth. The sons of Aaron, the high priest, were drunk and offered “strange incense” or smoke which the Lord had not commanded. They were instantly killed for defiling the temple of God.(Lev 10:1). To smoke cigarettes, weed and other habit-forming intoxicants is no different than what Nadab and Abihu did.
